query: Johor state election 2026 expected outcome, green wave PN Perikatan Nasional Malay voters, Barisan Nasional incumbency, Onn Hafiz popularity
answer:
I've completed the research and submitted the structured findings. Here's a summary of what I found on the 2026 Johor state election:

## Key Findings

**Timing & structure:** Polling is 11 July 2026 (early voting 7 July), following the 1 June dissolution by caretaker MB Onn Hafiz Ghazi. All 56 seats are contested; 29 needed for a majority; ~2.7M voters; 172 candidates with no walkovers.

**BN incumbency & Onn Hafiz:** BN is the clear favorite, holding 40/56 seats from its 2022 landslide. Onn Hafiz benefits from UMNO family legacy, a youthful/energetic image, expanded social spending, and a strong economy (Johor grew 6.4% in 2025 with RM110bn FDI). But BN raised its own bar by contesting all 56 seats alone — an internal survey projects only ~35 seats vs a 40–42 target, with urban minority seats as the weak spot.

**"Green wave"/PN Malay voters — notably weaker in Johor:** PN contests only 33 seats, and critically, **PAS and Bersatu have ended cooperation**, campaigning with separate machinery under a shared PN logo. Analysts expect this to split the Malay vote and *benefit BN*. Johor is UMNO's heartland where PN won just 3 seats in 2022.

**Fragmentation:** A messy field — 14 straight BN-PH fights, 27 three-cornered, 12 four-way, 3 five-way. BN and PH (federal partners) fight all 56 seats against each other; new **Parti Bersama** (Rafizi/Nik Nazmi defectors) debuts in 15 southern seats, potentially splitting the anti-BN vote.

**Trajectory (not a forecast):** Data favors BN retaining government; the open questions are whether it hits its 40-seat baseline or slips toward ~35, how much three-way splitting/Bersama erodes PH, and turnout.

The full prose report, key facts, sources, and quantitative data points have been delivered via the StructuredOutput tool.

key_facts: Johor 16th state election polls on 11 July 2026 (early voting 7 July); assembly dissolved 1 June 2026 by caretaker MB Onn Hafiz Ghazi (UMNO/BN).
All 56 assembly seats contested; 29 needed for majority; ~2.7 million eligible voters; 24,677 postal ballots issued; 172 candidates, no walkovers.
BN is the incumbent holding 40/56 seats from its 2022 landslide (2022: BN 40, PH 12, PN 3, MUDA 1).
An internal BN survey in late June 2026 projected only ~35 seats, below BN's own 40–42 seat target, citing urban minority (Chinese/Indian) seats and lingering corruption concerns.

The PN 'green wave' is weaker in Johor (UMNO heartland): PN contests only 33 seats and PAS and Bersatu have ended cooperation, running separate campaigns under the same PN logo — expected to split the Malay vote and benefit BN.
PN seat allocation: Bersatu 16, PAS 11, MIPP 5, Pejuang 1; Gerakan opted out over a seat dispute. PN won just 3 Johor seats in 2022.
Nomination configuration: 14 straight fights (BN vs PH), 27 three-cornered, 12 four-way, 3 five-cornered contests.
Both federal unity-government partners BN and PH contest all 56 seats against each other; BN lineup Umno 37/MCA 15/MIC 4, PH lineup PKR 20/Amanah 19/DAP 17.
New Parti Bersama (PKR defectors Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi) debuts in 15 seats in southern Johor; MUDA contests 4 — both may split the anti-BN vote.
Johor economy grew 6.4% in 2025 (fastest state) with RM110 billion FDI, boosting Onn Hafiz's development-focused campaign (JSSEZ, RTS Link).
Onn Hafiz benefits from UMNO family legacy (Onn Jaafar, Hussein Onn), youthful image, and social-assistance spending; he contests Malay-majority (66%) Machap seat.
12 marginal seats (2022 margins <10%) are key battlegrounds: Jementah, Kemelah, Bukit Kepong, Bukit Pasir, Serom, Maharani, Tenggaroh, Tiram, Johor Jaya, Perling, Kota Iskandar, Bukit Batu.
The election is framed as a test of Anwar's Madani unity government and a precursor to GE16; Negeri Sembilan votes shortly after on 1 August 2026.

data_points.metric: BN approximate votes per recent election
